Dear Ms. Cassady,
I have !evier^,ed the Political Reform Act of 1974 and I
found any prohj.bition against a candidate paying for
political activities and then being reimbursed trom his
I may be a candidaee next year, so I i{oulal like some
advise to avoid any problems as the reporting requirements.
The Lnstructions in use in 1986 for preparing Schedule E
included this statement! (Copy enclosed)
" The deposit of canpaign funds into an interest
bearing account, or the purchase of certificates
of deposit, stock, shares in a mutual bond or any
other asset which can be readily converted to
cash, should not be repo!ted as an expenditure,
and the conmitteers cash on hand should not be
reduced by the amount of the investnent. These
amounts should be incl,uded in the ncash on handn
fig u re."
I ilo not find a similar statement in the current
instructions in regard to purchasing stock or any other asset
that can be readily converted to cash.
OUESTION 1!
May I transfer canpaign funds from a bank accouot into a
stock narket funal sold by such firms as !.terrlll Lynch pierce
Fenner and Smith Inc. or Bateman Eichler Hil] and Richards Inc.
without violating section 85201?

whi.ch section applies.
If so, please
